name: Identity Resolution Hub — Zapier Native Blueprint
summary: Resolves people, account, owner, and channel identities across CRM, messaging, and meeting systems into a canonical identity layer so downstream workflows stop breaking on duplicate humans, alias drift, and ambiguous account ownership. Native-first Zapier blueprint that favors published custom apps, native app steps, and structured AI outputs over API Request, Webhooks, and Code-heavy builds.

official_references.label: Custom actions and API requests actions

native_connectors_required: Gmail, Microsoft Outlook, or Email by Zapier actions
Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook Calendar actions
Meeting-system native app or published custom Zapier app for transcript retrieval
Zapier Tables or Storage for state, routing maps, and dedupe keys
Approved webhook sink, database action, queue adapter, or downstream custom app action for publication
prohibited_patterns: Do not use raw API Requests or Custom Actions as the long-term production answer when a reusable custom Zapier app can be published
Do not use Webhooks, Code, Looping, Formatter, or Paths as the basis for a reusable published Zap template
Do not send unvalidated LLM text straight to Slack, Teams, email, calendar, or project-management actions
Do not hide routing or canonical data mapping inside opaque code when Tables, native apps, or custom-app actions can own it
template_constraints: Public Zap templates have product restrictions, so multi-surface workflows may need a blueprint bundle instead of one monolithic reusable Zap.
Use private or published custom Zapier apps for durable source-system integrations instead of repeated API Request steps.

trigger_model.recommended_surface: Sub-Zap orchestration via Zapier Tables, Interfaces, or a parent app trigger

targets: Webhook, queue, database, or downstream workflow sink
apps: Gmail, Microsoft Outlook, or Email by Zapier actions
Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook Calendar actions
Approved webhook sink, database action, queue adapter, or downstream custom app action for publication

blueprint_bundle.trigger: Sub-Zap orchestration via Zapier Tables, Interfaces, or a parent app trigger
native_steps: Native app step: Match Canonical Entities
AI step backed by a native LLM app returning strict JSON for AI Ambiguity Review
Native delivery step: database, queue, webhook, or downstream app action for Publish Canonical Identity Update
blueprint_bundle.notes: Keep the primary Zap focused on normalization, source access, and structured synthesis. Route secondary fan-out through additional native Zaps when template restrictions or retry requirements demand decomposition.

blueprint_bundle.trigger: Zapier Tables state change, Interfaces action, or an upstream custom app event
native_steps: Read delivery_payload and target metadata from structured fields
Gmail, Outlook, or Email by Zapier action
Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook Calendar action
Approved webhook sink, database action, queue adapter, or downstream custom app action
blueprint_bundle.notes: Use this secondary Zap when you need reusable delivery fan-out, fallback paths, or approval boundaries without falling back to Webhooks or Code steps.

ai_ambiguity_review.intent: AI Agent explains duplicate humans, merged subsidiaries, or alias conflicts before the identity graph is updated broadly.

deployment_notes: Use Zapier Tables or Storage for canonical keys, approval state, dedupe, and retry tracking.
Split orchestration and delivery into separate Zaps when parent-child reuse is needed.
Keep transport deterministic and reserve AI for classification, synthesis, and explanation.
